input.command:
kubectl get statefulset mongodb-replica -n mongodb -o jsonpath='{.spec.template.spec.containers[0].livenessProbe}' | jq .
echo "---readiness---"
kubectl get statefulset mongodb-replica -n mongodb -o jsonpath='{.spec.template.spec.containers[0].readinessProbe}' | jq .
echo "---pods---"
kubectl get pods -n mongodb -o wide
echo "---events---"
kubectl get events -n mongodb --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -30

input.command:
kubectl top nodes 2>&1
echo "---top pods---"
kubectl top pods -n mongodb 2>&1
echo "---describe statefulset probes---"
kubectl get statefulset mongodb-replica -n mongodb -o json | python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); c=d['spec']['template']['spec']['containers'][0]; print(json.dumps({'liveness':c.get('livenessProbe'),'readiness':c.get('readinessProbe'),'resources':c.get('resources')}, indent=2))"

content.text: Found it — the readiness/liveness probes were patched to unreasonably tight values (`timeoutSeconds: 1`, `failureThreshold: 2`), which isn't enough time for `mongosh` (a Node.js process) to spawn and connect, causing a crash loop even though mongod itself is healthy. Let me confirm current pod state, then fix the probes.

input.command:
kubectl get pods -n mongodb
echo "--- test how long mongosh ping actually takes ---"
kubectl exec -n mongodb mongodb-replica-2 -- /bin/sh -c 'time (mongosh --quiet "mongodb://admin-user:$(cat /etc/admin-secret/password)@localhost:27017/admin" --eval "db.adminCommand({ping:1})")' 2>&1

mongodb-replica-2   0/1     Running   1 (16s ago)   88s

/bin/sh: 1: Syntax error: word unexpected (expecting ")")
command terminated with exit code 2

summary: kubectl exec -n mongodb mongodb-replica-2 -- /bin/sh -c 'START=$(date +%s%N); mongosh --quiet "mongodb://admin-user:$(cat /etc/admin-secret/password)@localhost:27017/admin" --eval "db.adminCommand({ping:1})"; END=$(date +%s%N); echo "elapsed_ms=$(( (END-START)/1000000 ))"' 2>&1

content.text: Confirmed: a simple `ping` via `mongosh` takes ~3.7s under current load, but the probe `timeoutSeconds` was set to 1 — guaranteed failure. I'll patch the StatefulSet with realistic probe timing.

input.command:
kubectl get pods -n mongodb -o wide
kubectl rollout status statefulset/mongodb-replica -n mongodb --timeout=240s
